feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue Consuming OpenAPI OData from SAP Datasphere in Node.js – Access Token Returns Login Link Inste
entry.content:
Hello, community!

I’m working on a project where I need to consume data from SAP Datasphere via OData using OpenAPI in Node.js. I've already set up an application to obtain credentials, successfully made the authentication request, and retrieved the access token.

The issue now is that when I use the access token to pull the data, instead of receiving the JSON data as expected, I get a login link in the response. I've double-checked the request structure and authentication configurations, but I still can't resolve this issue.

Has anyone encountered this before or could suggest any necessary adjustments to get the API to return data in JSON format?

Any guidance or practical examples would be greatly appreciated!

Code:

const { executeHttpRequest } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/core');
const axios = require('axios');

async function getAccessToken(clientId, clientSecret) {
    const tokenUrl = 'https://url_url_url.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token'; // URL de token correta
    const response = await axios.post(tokenUrl,
      new URLSearchParams({
        client_id: clientId,
        client_secret: clientSecret,
        grant_type: 'client_credentials'
      }),
      {
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' }
      }
    );
    return response.data.access_token;
  }
 
  async function executeRequest(accessToken) {
    const response = await axios.get(
      'https://url_url_url.us10.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/DW_NEL/AM_MOV_V5/AM_MOV_V5',
      {
        headers: {
          Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`
        }
      }
    );
    console.log(response.data);
  }

(async () => {
  try {
    const accessToken = await getAccessToken('Client ID', 'Client Secret');
    await executeRequest(accessToken);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Erro na execução da API:', error);
  }
})();


RETURN:
<html><head><link rel="shortcut icon" href="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" /><script>document.cookie="fragmentAfterLogin="+encodeURIComponent(location.hash)+";path=/;Secure;SameSite=None";document.cookie="locationAfterLogin="+encodeURIComponent(location.href.split('#')[0].substring(location.href.split('#')[0].indexOf(location.host) + location.host.length))+";path=/;Secure;SameSite=None";document.cookie="signature=J3wPRHL%2FOV5vrzdIeTdo1n2w9%2F8%3D;path=/;Secure;SameSite=None";location="https://url_url_url.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=sb-approuter-sac-sacus10!t655&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Furl_url_url.us10.hcs.cloud.sap%2Fsso%2Flogin%2Fcallback&state=b9df485-5eb-46ed-9bb3-302bd2264"</script></head></html>

entry.title: SSO Azure AD unauthorized issue
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I would like to set up SSO from Azure AD in my Datasphere.

I have set up everything from the Identity Provider (Azure AD) side.
and also have set everything in Datasphere.

But whenever I get to "Step 4: Confirm that the mapping is working."

I open the link in a new incognito tab and I sign in with SSO normally but then I get unauthorized.
which tells me that the SSO works but I still need authorization.

Can you please direct me the way I can solve this?

Best

Sam

tcodataspherefree.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap

entry.title: Level Based Hierarchy description not working on lower level
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am currently working with a Custom Customer hierarchy, and I am facing a strange thing when using it.

I have built a level-based hierarchy dimension, and I am using it via an association with my fact view.

When I am previewing the data in the analytic model or in SAC I have a strange bug. When using the Customer dimension without the hierarchy (flat presentation) I have the customer ID and Description working fine. 

If I select the hierarchy, I now loose the description for the customer but it's still working for the other level of the hierarchy. Only the description from the last level is lost, so in my case, I loose the customer description. It now show the ID in the description.

Do any of you have seen something like that?

Thanks

entry.title: How can we analyzing sql view execution in SAP Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We have a SQL View in Datasphere and we import the output of this SQL View to an Import Model in SAC. We have done some work on SQL View. After this work, the duration of the related job of this Import Model in SAC has increased a lot. We want to analyze this. Is there a feature similar to Plan Visualizer in Eclipse in Datasphere or Database Explorer?

entry.title: Inquiry about issues stopping replication flow.
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I have been facing an issue with a replication flow that I have been trying to stop for a week, but without success. I would like to know if anyone has experienced something similar and could help me understand the possible causes of this problem.

I have tried several solutions, but the flow remains active. Could you suggest what else I might check or if there are any specific steps I should follow to resolve this issue?

I appreciate any guidance or advice you can offer.

Best regards

entry.title: Datasphere repeat business unit for all dates
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to achieve below scenario in target view, new records should be created for each date for each business unit. So target view should have record for each date and business unit combination. There are other dimensions as well which should not be considered for combination.

 

Regards,

VK

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - how to externally trigger replication/data flow
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We wanted to trigger Replication flow or Data flow externally via API call through a different scheduling tool (that runs other jobs in the client landscape).
Datasphere has its own scheduling and monitoring tool but we wanted to leverage the current client scheduling tool to run and monitor the flows. Is there a way to do this?

Thanks,

entry.title: Copy widgets or pages between Stories when using aDatasphere Live Connection
entry.content:
Hello,

I've learned that it is not possible to copy widgets or pages between Stories when using a Datasphere Live Connection.     

Details in this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3128880 

Here is an Influence request that I have created.  

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/334757 

If this is something that you would find useful, please vote! 

Thank you

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Datasphere

entry.title: SAP Datasphere connection with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

This is my first post, wish me luck ! 

I am looking for a way to connect SAP Datasphere # and  SAP Commerce Cloud #. We are planning to get data from SAP Commerce Cloud to SAP Datasphere and modeling at SAP Datasphere to report in SAP BusinessObjects WebI.

It seems like a two way to do it;

1-) Create a RESTful API and establish a connection

2-) Connect SAP Commerce Cloud to a MSSQL server and push the Commerce Cloud table data to it and update in a constant periods of time. Then connect the MSSQL server to SAP Datasphere.

Any advice for a third way or any comments on these two ways ?

The Best, Onur

entry.title: Issue with Loading Data Source from SAP S/4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere via Replication Flow
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am facing an issue while trying to load data from SAP S/4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere through the Replication Flow. Despite following the steps, no data source appears in the selection screen (as shown in the screenshot).

However, when I attempt to load the same data source using the Remote Table option, the data source (e.g., 2LIS_11_VAHDR) is visible (as shown in the screenshot).

I suspect there might be some configuration or access issue specific to ODP in the replication flow. I would appreciate it if you could share your experiences or insights to resolve this problem.

Thank you for your support!

Best regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: SAC; Measure Descirption; Dynamic Text (Variable); DataSphere Data Model
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need to dynamically adjust the Description of measures; The Measures are Restricted Measures, created in Datasphere, they're having an offset on CALMONTH, so you can imagine something like this:
Column1: Sales, CALMONTH_offset = -1; 
Column2: Sales; CALMONTH_offset = 0;
Column3: Sales; CALMONTH_offset = +1
etc.
Is there ANY way I can somehow dynamically adjust the Description of the Restricted Measures? (DataSphere and SAC are both options). Open for wild ideas as well
SAP Datasphere SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Does SAP DataSphere have a "Business Content" option like SAP BW for ETL processes?
entry.content:
Hi

In SAP BW, there is an option called "Install from Business Content," which provides a built-in module for the entire ETL (Extraction, Transformation, Loading) process for each InfoProviders . SD,MM,FI.... My question is, does SAP DataSphere have a similar feature where we can easily download and install readily available business content for ETL processes? If so, how can we leverage it for quick deployment?


Kumar

entry.title: Problem with Geo-coodinates colums
entry.content:
I request support, I managed to use the Geo-coodinates colums from the Datasphere and now when I try to view it again from the Datasphere the following message is displayed: Right now, there are no visible columns in the table. Please select the columns you need in the table settings.

The creation of the Location dimension is done as follows:
1. A graphical view called CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_1 with the columns latitude and longitude is available.


2. Created a CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2 view with Semantic Usage "Dimension"


3. A Calculated Columns was created with Data Type hana. ST_Geometry from CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2

4. Created a CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_3 view with Semantic Usage "Fact" and with Associations to CRM_PropiedadeCroquiCliente_2 by idparceiro


5. An Analytic Model was created CRM_PropiedadeCroquiClienteFinal_V1

entry.title: python script that uses oauth2.0 with 'api access' without manuel login to fetch data via odata?
entry.content:
Python script to get Datasphere data via odata api:

I have seen some guides to create oauth2.0 client and make rest calls to datasphere odata apis via Postman. However, that  oauth2.0 client uses the 'interactive' purpose option with a callback uri. That means i have to manually login on browser or open the link to get the first token and then continue querying via the odata api. This method works fine, however, it cannot be used for production script that pulls data from a Datasphere view every day.

For this reason, I tried with a oauthclient2.0 with 'api access' purpose and 'client_credentials'  as authorization, in order to avoid manual login. With the client_id and secret, i am able to call the token_url and receive a token. However, when i use this token to query any view or table in datasphere, it returns the login page html elements as text in the response.  
So my question is it possible to somehow avoid manual login and run a python script locally or in Azure cloud Function to get data from Datasphere via odata apis. 

@Asim_Munshi @gustavokath

entry.title: Issue where standard CDS View cannot be imported in Datasphere
entry.content:
Thank you for viewing.

As part of my investigation into Datasphere's hierarchy function, I am currently trying to reproduce the steps in the blog below on my actual device, but I am experiencing an issue where the CDS View related to G/L account information starting with "I_GLACCOUNT" in the blog is not displayed on Datasphere's remote table import screen. (Figure 1)

SAP Datasphere: Introducing the Directory Hierarchy - SAP Community 

 (図１)

 

By referring to the blog below, we have investigated the settings required for the target CDS View and confirmed that the following two conditions are met.
How to use CDS views that can extract data - SAP Community 
・The annotation "@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled" is set to "true"
・"ISSAPRELEASEDVIEW" in I_DATAEXTRACTIONENABLEDVIEW is set to X (⇒It is a released CDS view that can extract data)

However, one thing that struck me while researching the above was that the CDS View starting with "I_GR" shown in the image in (Figure 1) was displayed on Datasphere even though "ISSAPRELEASEDVIEW" was not set to X. (The annotation "@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled" was set to "true".)

 

I suspect that there is some setting that I've overlooked. if anyone knows how to solve this issue, I would appreciate your help.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: OData API request not working when Datasphere is set with SSO
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I have an OData URL that I use to get data from my Analytical Model in Datasphere:

the URL is:  https://tcodataspherefree.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/TCO_DATA_MODEL/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering?$top=10

It works just fine if I use it in my browser after I sign in with SSO.

But when I want to call it through a python code, after setting up OAuth on Datasphere, I am not getting any data back from the URL. I assume it is because the SSO is set.

Is there any way to get around that?

Many thanks

Sam

entry.title: Sharepoint CSV-File integration in Datasphere's Data Flow leads to an error
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently trying to establish a connection between SAP Datasphere and a customer's SharePoint.

To do this, we followed the instructions described in the following blog entry...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/accessing-sharepoint-files-from-datasphere-using-btp-open-connectors/ba-p/13917779

...and successfully created the Integration Suite service, along with the SharePoint connector provided by the Open Connectors.

Here is the configuration of the Open Connectors pointing to Sharepoint:

 

Unfortunately, we have not yet been able to display a file in the Datasphere.

To do this, we created a "test.csv" with a handful of data, which is located in the "Documents/Datasphere_Test" directory.

We can already see this folder structure in the Dataflow of the Datasphere:

 

But as soon as we drag and drop the "test.csv" file in, we get the following error message:

 

The error message displayed, shows that the "test.csv" cannot be found.

The Open Connectors uses the following URL call, which leads to the error:

<company>.sharepoint.com/_api/web/GetFolderByServerRelativeUrl('Freigegebene%20Dokumente/Datasphere_Test/test.csv')

 

We have already experimented a bit here and tested via the browser that the file can be found via the following URL:

<company>.sharepoint.com/_api/Web/GetFileByServerRelativePath(decodedurl='/Freigegebene%20Dokumente/Datasphere_Test/test.csv')

We assume that we still need to define something in the Open Connectors configuration?

Is there perhaps a challenge with URL coding or something similar?

We would be grateful for any relevant information here.

Additional challenge:
The above named blog entry aims to ensure that we have access to the top level of the SharePoint. In the future, however, we also want to access sites, such as the site “Datalake” that we created here:

 

Here, too, the question arises  what we need to adjust the Open Connectors (or perhaps also in Sharepoint) so that we can also consume files that exist in the 'datalake' site.

 

Our attempt to adjust the Sharepoint site address in the form

<company>.sharepoint.com/sites/datalake

led to authentication errors.

 

If we leave the Sharepoint site address untouched (<company>.sharepoint.com), we can authenticate, but we cannot see the data structures of the site “datalake”.

 

Changing the “Document Library” property to

/sites/datalake/

was also unsuccessful.

 

Does anyone here have experience of how we need to adjust our configuration(s)?

 

Many thanks in advance,

M. Spyker

entry.title: Schedule on fix hour replication flow based on S/4HANA CDS View
entry.content:
Hi expert,

We are implemented a Datasphere project for our customer and facing an issue where we were not able to schedule the delta load for a replication flow.

We can launch init + Delta, select delta intervall (for expample every 24h), but not choose à fix hour during the night.

Do you know if this function will be available in datasphere ?

Thanks a lot,

Regards, Bruno

entry.title: Import of Package Sales Analysis failed - Status Objects Pending
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to import the Business Content Package "Sales Analysis for SAP S/4Hana and SAP S/4 Hana Cloud" into SAP Datasphere.

I already imported the space, connection, tables, and views successfully. Now that I created the replication flow and ran it successfully, I wanted to import the Analytic Models.

So I just selected the models in import options. But the import fails with the following messages (I also tried to select the space + the analytic models the second time):

 

Thanks in advance

Regards

Maike

entry.title: Tableau desktop user into allowlist IP's in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Datasphere community,

We have a use case where different tableau desktop users need to allowlist Ip's to connect to views in datasphere. 

Is there a better solution to allow list Ip's where users will be connecting from different locations and dont have to allowlist each time they change the location?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
